data.title: Upstart adds Cash Line revolving credit for consumers
data.description:
Upstart (UPST), the lending platform used by banks and credit unions, introduced Cash Line,  a revolving line of credit that gives consumers access to cash as needed, the company said on Tuesday.

Cash Line offers an alternative to "unreliable and often predatory short-term options currently available," such as payday loans. Traditional cash advance apps often approve consumers for far less than they promise, add hidden fees, and provide unpredictable access, the company said.

Upstart's (UPST) new offering provides:

  • A guaranteed minimum of $200 for all approved consumers;
  • An up to $5,000 revolving line, more than 5x that of its leading competitors;
  • Approved lines are never reduced provided that program requirements are met;
  • No extra fees for expedited access;
  • Customized repayment options;
  • Pricing — $10 monthly membership for lines up to $500, plus APR of 5%-36% for draws exceeding $500.

Consumers can join the waitlist for early access to Cash Line's beta, starting Tuesday, before it's widely available with all features later in 2026, the company said.

"We are rapidly building a one-stop shop for all flavors of credit where guaranteed best rates are available 24/7 in a matter of minutes," said Upstart Co-Founder and CEO Dave Girouard. "Cash Line offers reliable access to money in the moments that matter the most."

Upstart (UPST) stock rose 3.5%  in Tuesday midday trading.

data.title: Ford plans to target the mass market with $30K EVs and low-cost electric truck
data.description:
Ford Motor (F) is overhauling its electric vehicle strategy after large losses, shifting away from big, expensive EVs toward smaller, more affordable models and a broader push into hybrids and energy storage. The goal is to bring to the market EVs with a starting price of $30K.

As part of the reset, Ford (F) is also expected to release a 2028 model year mid-size electric truck in 2027 that could be named Ranchero. Last August, Ford (F) filed a U.S. trademark application for the Ranchero name. The filing's broad description allows Ford (F) to use the name on an electric pickup positioned under the F-150 Lightning, aligned with its new low-cost EV platform for compact/mid-size trucks. Notably, the new EV truck is slated to be Ford's (F) first vehicle on its new Universal Electric Vehicle platform.

Ford (F) noted in a press release that part of its new EV strategy is obsessing over the vehicle as a system to get more miles out of a smaller battery and radically simplifying the system to reduce the number of parts so the company can deliver a new family of affordable electric vehicles to driveways around the world. "For the first time, customers will experience a fully electric vehicle charging ecosystem designed in-house by Ford using our own software. That means the hardware in the vehicle, including the bi-directional charging capabilities, comes from a team directly integrated into the one working on the platform and vehicle products," highlighted Ford (F).

data.title: Franklin Electric outlines $2.17B–$2.24B sales guidance for 2026 with margin expansion focus through value acceleration
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Franklin Electric Co., Inc. (FELE) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Joseph Ruzynski highlighted "strong Q4 and full year results in all segments," reporting that "sales were up 5.4% and segment operating income was up 9.6% for the full year, each representing high points for Franklin in both revenue and segment operating income." He emphasized volume growth, strong price realization, and a healthy order book and backlog for 2026. Ruzynski also stated, "Our cash conversion was 126%, representing our third year of cash conversion over 120%."
  • Ruzynski described the Water treatment business as a key growth driver, exiting 2025 at $200 million in sales, with an operating margin improvement of over 400 basis points. He noted the Distribution business has grown to over $700 million, with operating margin up 210 basis points in 2025. Ruzynski introduced the Value Acceleration Office, using "80-20, smart AI and process engineering to streamline our portfolio, create powerful and simple internal systems, and manage costs more effectively."
  • He reported $120 million in acquisitions and $160 million in share buybacks during 2025, and emphasized increased capital spending to support growth ambitions.
  • CFO Jennifer Wolfenbarger stated, "Our full year 2025 fully diluted earnings per share was $3.22 versus $3.86 for 2024," noting a pension settlement charge that negatively impacted EPS by $0.91. She said, "Adjusted diluted earnings per share was $4.14 in 2025 versus adjusted 2024 of $3.92, an increase of 6%." Wolfenbarger also reported, "Fourth quarter 2025 consolidated sales were $506.9 million, a year-over-year increase of 4.4%."

Outlook

  • Wolfenbarger announced that "the company expects its full year 2026 sales to be in the range of $2.17 billion and $2.24 billion, and an adjusted EPS to be in the range of $4.40 to $4.60." She emphasized, "This puts our midpoint sales growth at just over 3% and our midpoint EPS at approximately 9%."
  • CEO Ruzynski projected Water business organic growth in the "3% to 5% range," with volume and price contributing, and noted the Energy business growth is expected to be "over 3-ish percent," with a heavier volume component. He also indicated Distribution segment growth of "3% to 4%," with a similar price-volume mix as last year.

Financial Results

  • Wolfenbarger reported, "Fourth quarter 2025 consolidated sales were $506.9 million, a year-over-year increase of 4.4%." She detailed that gross profit for Q4 was $171.5 million, with gross margin at 33.8%. "SG&A expenses were $119.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2025, compared to $117.8 million in the prior year," with cost improvement actions cited as a driver for a 70 basis point improvement in SG&A as a percent of sales.
  • Consolidated operating income for Q4 was $51.6 million, up $8.6 million from the prior year, and the operating income margin was 10.2%. For the full year, consolidated sales reached $2.1 billion, with operating income of $269 million and a margin of 12.6%.
  • Segment highlights included Global Water Systems Q4 sales up 4.3%, Energy Systems sales up 9%, and Distribution sales up 3%. The Distribution segment's operating income margin improved by 300 basis points to 3.3% in Q4.
  • The company ended 2025 with a cash balance of $99.7 million and $30 million outstanding under its revolving credit agreement. Net cash flows from operating activities were $239 million for 2025.

Q&A

  • Matt Summerville, D.A. Davidson: Asked about the organic outlook and price-volume mix in guidance. Ruzynski responded, "For the Water business, we're looking at a number that's probably in the 3% to 5% range... a good blend between volume and price." For Energy, he said growth is "over 3-ish percent... a little more volume than price."
  • Summerville: Inquired about key end markets and geography in Water. Ruzynski noted stabilization in previously weak HVAC and Mexican markets, with "no spots that are going to have pronounced weakness."
  • Ryan Connors, Northcoast Research: Requested details on HVAC headwinds. Wolfenbarger explained the softness was "really isolated to the back end of Q4" and expected normalization in January.
  • Connors: Questioned dewatering growth deceleration. Ruzynski attributed it to "a little bit of a pause at the end of the year," but indicated a healthy backlog and positive outlook.
  • Connors: Asked about Energy Systems margin headwinds from tariffs. Ruzynski said, "We knew there was going to be a little bit of a timing issue there... margins bounce back. We expect the Energy margins to be up slightly this year versus last year."
  • Bryan Blair, Oppenheimer: Sought color on Barnes and PumpEng integration and M&A pipeline. Ruzynski said, "PumpEng deal is on... ahead of track... Barnes deal... we're probably behind a little bit there just because the... shrinking environment in Mexico... it looks like it's stabilized."
  • Blair: Asked about Value Acceleration Office. Ruzynski described it as "more than just cost improvement" and expects "some of that baked in our plan for expanded EPS here."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were neutral to slightly positive, focusing on guidance details, margin recovery in Energy, and M&A integration, with no indications of skepticism or pressing tone.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, with phrases such as "we are making it more resilient every day" and "we expect some good readout this year."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence remained steady, while analysts maintained a similar, information-seeking tone.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company shifted to providing adjusted EPS guidance for 2026, moving away from GAAP reported EPS.
  • Guidance for sales and EPS in 2026 was raised above the prior full-year outlook range for 2025.
  • Margin improvement efforts, particularly in Distribution and Water Treatment, were highlighted in greater detail, with the Value Acceleration Office introduced as a new strategic initiative.
  • Analysts' focus remained on growth drivers, margin recovery, and M&A integration, with additional interest in price-volume dynamics and geographic performance.
  • Management’s tone and confidence remained strong quarter over quarter, while analysts' questions did not indicate a significant shift in sentiment.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tariff impacts as a margin headwind in Energy Systems, with mitigation through price increases.
  • HVAC softness and Mexican market weakness were discussed but described as stabilizing.
  • Integration of recent acquisitions, particularly Barnes, was noted as slower due to economic conditions in Mexico.
  • Analysts highlighted potential timing issues with price increases and margin recovery in Energy.

Final Takeaway

Franklin Electric delivered strong Q4 and full year 2025 results, highlighted by robust sales and operating income growth, significant margin improvement initiatives, and healthy cash conversion. The company introduced the Value Acceleration Office to drive future efficiency and margin gains, while 2026 guidance signals continued growth in both sales and adjusted EPS. Management’s outlook remains positive across all segments, with ongoing investments in innovation, acquisitions, and cost structure improvements supporting confidence in the company’s long-term strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Fears around a market rotation into broader risk-off are overdone – HSBC
data.description:
Fears that the current market rotation will spiral into a broader “risk-off” environment are significantly overblown, according to HSBC Chief Multi-Asset Strategist Max Kettner.

In an interview with CNBC, Kettner noted that despite notable pullbacks in certain sectors, the S&P 500 (SP500) remains near record highs, suggesting the underlying bull market remains intact.

Kettner pointed to historical precedent to support his view, explaining that similar high-beta momentum unwinds over the past 10 to 15 years have not led to widespread market declines.

“This fear around the rotation transpiring into broader risk-off really appears a little bit overdone,” he said. These past episodes did not result in significant equity selloffs, credit spread widening, or major currency moves.

The recent volatility in AI stocks has actually improved market technicals by cleaning up the stretched positioning that developed late last year, he added.

Kettner noted that a minor sell signal from December has entirely disappeared, leaving sentiment and positioning much cleaner.

“That really should be still pretty supportive for dip buying,” he added.

For investors searching for opportunities, Kettner highlighted several sectors within the U.S. equity space that he called the “lower end of the K-shaped economy.” His preferred areas include retail (XRT), transport (IYT), (XTN), homebuilders (XHB), and regional banks (IAT), (KRE)—sectors that have only started to outperform in the past two to two and a half months and remain attractive going forward.

Internationally, Kettner remains bullish on European and Japanese banks despite narrowing valuation discounts compared to U.S. equities.

“Earnings can grow even more from here just with the yield curve remaining the same,” he explained, noting that steep yield curves in both regions continue to provide fundamental tailwinds for the trade.

On currency markets, Kettner dismissed concerns about lasting damage to the U.S. dollar (DXY), noting that the greenback has remained range-bound between 97 and 100 on the DXY index for roughly nine months.

This suppressed volatility in foreign exchange and fixed income markets creates a favorable backdrop for emerging market investments across both equities and fixed income.

data.title: Chevron-led consortium signs gas exploration deal in Greece
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) said Monday it signed lease agreements with Greece's government which will enable exploration of four offshore blocks, expanding its presence in the eastern Mediterranean.

The agreement allows Chevron (CVX) to lead the search for gas in four deepwater blocks south of the Peloponnese peninsula and the island of Crete, stretching across 47K sq km.

The consortium, in which Chevron (CVX) holds a 70% operating interest and Helliniq Energy a 30% stake, was selected following an international call for tender launched by the government in 2025.

Under the lease terms, the consortium will carry out 2D and 3D seismic surveys during the first exploration phase to evaluate hydrocarbon potential.

Chevron (CVX) already has assets in the Mediterranean region that include two gas producing fields offshore Israel and the Aphrodite gas field in development offshore Cyprus; in Egypt, Chevron is operator of two Egyptian exploration blocks and is in a non-operated joint venture in the Mediterranean Sea.

data.title: Tesla Austin robotaxis more accident-prone than humans -- electrek
data.description:
Tesla’s (TSLA) Austin, TX robotaxis have been involved in 14 crashes since June 2025, with 5 accidents occurring in December and January, raising concerns that the autonomously driven vehicle may be more accident-prone than humans.

Data compiled by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration’s Standing General Order incident report database for automated driving systems includes a collision between a Tesla robotaxi and a fixed object at 17 mph, another with a heavy truck at 4 mph, and two backup collisions at very low speeds.

According to electrek, Tesla (TSLA) redacts information on these collisions and submits limited details as “confidential business information” in contrast to competitors. Additionally, a July crash was upgraded to include an injury versus an earlier submission that the crash resulted in property damage only.

By extrapolating mileage data from Tesla’s (TSLA) fourth quarter earnings report, electrek estimates the company’s robotaxis have driven ~800K miles by mid-January. With 14 crashes on the books, that translates into 1 crash every 57K miles. By comparison, NHTSA data from 2022 shows human drivers will experience one minor or major crash (excluding fender benders) every 500K miles. By this measure, electrek suggests the Tesla (TSLA) robotaxi is 8 times more likely to crash than a human driver. 

Tesla (TSLA) currently operates approximately 45 robotaxis in Austin, Texas.

data.title: Gilead in up to $1.5B deal with China's Genhouse Bio for tumor candidate
data.description:
• Gilead Sciences (GILD) is paying Suzhou, China-based Genhouse Bio $80M upfront to gain exclusive worldwide rights to the latter's MAT2A inhibitor GH31, under investigation to treat various tumor types.
  • Terms call for Genhouse to potentially receive up to $1.45B in milestone payments, as well as tiered double-digit royalties based on net sales. 
  • Gilead is responsible for global development, regulatory submissions, and future commercialization
  • GH31 has IND approval in the US and China.

data.title: Top 10 bearish Infotech stocks with highest year-over-year capex growth
data.description:
As the market grapples with AI-related worries, below is a list of 10 Information Technology stocks with Sell or Strong Sell Quant ratings ranked according to their year-over-year capital expenditure (Capex) growth percentage.

The list is topped by GoDaddy (GDDY), with a Y/Y Capex growth percentage of 93.83%. Varonis Systems (VRNS) and Manhattan Associates (MANH) are next, with The Hackett Group (HCKT) and Amplitude (AMPL) rounding out the rest of the top five.

Other companies ranking high on the list include Alpha and Omega Semiconductor (AOSL) and Guidewire Software (GWRE). N-able (NABL), Sprout Social (SPT), and Vertex (VERX) complete the top ten.

Here is the list:

  1. GoDaddy (GDDY), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 93.83%

  2. Varonis Systems (VRNS), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 88.65%

  3. Manhattan Associates (MANH), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 78.18%

  4. The Hackett Group (HCKT), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 73.71%

  5. Amplitude (AMPL), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 72.76%

  6. Alpha and Omega Semiconductor (AOSL), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 61.10%

  7. Guidewire Software (GWRE), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 57.50%

  8. N-able (NABL), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 51.72%

  9. Sprout Social (SPT), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 46.41%

  10. Vertex (VERX), Y/Y Capex growth percentage: 46.32%

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Prediction markets forecast a 22-day DHS shutdown as odds of prolonged standoff ease
data.description:
Prediction market traders are dialing back expectations for an extended government shutdown, even as uncertainty lingers ahead of Congress’ return to Washington on February 23.

According to data from Kalshi, participants now estimate a partial Department of Homeland Security shutdown will last roughly 22 days. That marks a decline from last week’s projected peak of around 30 days, signaling a modest improvement in sentiment surrounding a potential resolution. 

Still, market pricing suggests traders see a meaningful risk that funding negotiations could stretch well beyond the near term. Probabilities remain heavily skewed toward a shutdown lasting at least several weeks, though the odds taper off as the projected duration extends deeper into the calendar. 

Below is the latest probability breakdown, ranked from highest to lowest likelihood according to Kalshi data: 

  • 99% — At least 5 days
  • 99% — At least 7 days 
  • 98% — At least 10 days
  • 83% — At least 15 days 
  • 62% — At least 20 days 
  • 48% — At least 25 days
  • 39% — At least 30 days 
  • 28% — At least 35 days 
  • 19% — At least 40 days
  • 12% — At least 43 days 
  • 9% — At least 50 days 
  • 8% — At least 60 days 
  • 5% — At least 90 days

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).
